name: school_bus_routing_bridge_weight
premise: Darren Cho, transportation director for Westfield Schools, must decide whether to disable a new bus routing system on the first day of winter exams. The system has shortened routes by 18% and reduced late arrivals for students who depend on school breakfast. A dispatcher discovers that the software sometimes routes full buses across a rural bridge whose weight limit was updated in a county PDF but not imported into the routing database. The bridge has passed visual inspection, but the updated limit reflects hidden corrosion discovered last month. Disabling the routing system will delay hundreds of students, including those taking state exams that affect graduation eligibility. Keeping it active with verbal instructions to drivers may miss substitute drivers who rely entirely on navigation prompts.

agents.name: Darren Cho
agents.identity: I am the school transportation director responsible for student safety and reliable exam-day access.
agents.goal: I want students to arrive safely and on time without gambling on an infrastructure blindspot.

agents.name: Marta Ruiz
agents.identity: I am the senior dispatcher who identified the bridge weight-limit mismatch in the routing database.
agents.goal: I want routes corrected before any full bus is sent across a bridge whose safe load is uncertain.

choices: I will disable the routing system and manually reroute all buses around the bridge, accepting widespread exam delays.
I will keep the routing system active and issue urgent driver instructions to avoid the bridge, accepting the risk that substitute drivers may miss the warning.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Darren Cho must decide whether to preserve exam-day punctuality or disable routing around an unverified bridge risk. What does Darren Cho do?
